title: Google Cloud Security 使用 Instruqt 平台，在 2026 年 Google Next 大會上為 150 多名從業人員提供代理型人工智能培訓
author: Globe Newswire
description: Google Cloud Security partnered with Instruqt to deliver hands-on agentic AI training to over 150 practitioners at Google Next 2026. Participants built personalized Google Cloud Vertex AI environments and developed their own AI agents in sandbox environments, addressing the industry-wide gap between rapid AI feature releases and customer adoption rates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Google Cloud Security demonstrated proactive approach to addressing AI adoption challenges by implementing large-scale, hands-on training programs. This shows commitment to customer success and reducing the adoption gap for complex AI products.

Google Cloud Security demonstrated proactive approach to addressing AI adoption challenges by implementing large-scale, hands-on training programs. This shows commitment to customer success and reducing the adoption gap for complex AI products.
MongoDB is mentioned as an existing Instruqt customer but has no direct involvement in the reported event. Mention is purely contextual regarding Instruqt's client base.
Elastic is mentioned as an existing Instruqt customer but has no direct involvement in the reported event. Mention is purely contextual regarding Instruqt's client base.

title: Google Cloud 보안 부문, Instruqt 플랫폼 활용해 ‘Google Next 2026’서 150명 이상 대상 에이전틱 AI 교육 진행
author: Globe Newswire
description: Google Cloud Security leveraged Instruqt's hands-on learning platform to conduct one of the largest practical Agentic AI training programs at Google Next 2026, with over 150 practitioners building custom AI agents in dedicated Vertex AI sandbox environments. The initiative addresses the growing adoption gap between rapid AI feature releases and actual customer implementation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Google Cloud Security successfully executed a large-scale, innovative AI training program demonstrating commitment to customer enablement and addressing the AI adoption gap. The initiative showcases Google's leadership in practical AI education and customer support.

title: Emerging AI-Driven Threats Prompt Renewed Focus on Enterprise Cybersecurity
author: Equity Insider
description: Following Anthropic's exposure of Claude Mythos, a frontier AI model that autonomously exploits zero-day vulnerabilities, the cybersecurity sector faces renewed pressure to modernize defenses. The incident triggered a 4.5% drop in the Global X Cybersecurity ETF. Major players including SentinelOne, Elastic, Rapid7, and Broadcom are advancing AI-powered security solutions, while quantum-safe encryption adoption remains critical as 91% of businesses lack formal migration roadmaps despite tightening compliance deadlines.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Expanded strategic partnership with Alphabet/Google Cloud across three regions; AI-native Singularity Platform positioned to replace legacy security tools; protects nearly one-fifth of Fortune 500 companies

Achieved FedRAMP High authorization unlocking access to sensitive government workloads; growing federal footprint with CISA collaboration and GSA discount agreement; supports cloud-native security operations

Acquired Kenzo Security to advance autonomous security investigations; customers report 94% reduction in investigation time and 100% alert coverage; enhances managed detection and response capabilities

Launched Symantec CBX unified detection and response platform combining Symantec and Carbon Black technologies; 85% of incident flags now carry AI-powered predictions; targets under-resourced security teams

title: Kettle Hill Loads Up With 161,000 Shares of RH Worth $28.9 Million
author: Will Healy
description: Kettle Hill Capital Management acquired 161,122 shares of RH (Restoration Hardware) valued at $28.87 million, making it the fund's third-largest position. Despite RH's stock being down 46.1% over the past year, the investment appears opportunistic given the company's 10% revenue growth and 64% net income increase in the first nine months of fiscal 2025, along with an improved forward P/E ratio of 20.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite significant recent stock decline (-46.1% YoY), the company shows strong operational fundamentals with 10% revenue growth and 64% net income growth in fiscal 2025. The forward P/E of 20 suggests potential recovery, and a major fund's new investment indicates institutional confidence in a turnaround opportunity.
Mentioned only as Kettle Hill's largest holding ($29.69M, 6.6% of AUM). No specific news or analysis provided about the company itself.
Mentioned only as Kettle Hill's second-largest holding ($29.06M, 6.5% of AUM). No specific news or analysis provided about the company itself.
